response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack developer with strong software craftsmanship values, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary and recent expertise is in TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies — not Java or Kotlin. While Java appears in his skills list and he used it briefly at Altom (2016–2018) for robotic arms and a mobile orchestration tool, there is no evidence of deep JVM knowledge, Spring ecosystem experience, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any enterprise Java framework work. The role requires exactly this expertise as a core competency, not a peripheral one. His IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments was in C/C++ for a C IDE, not JVM tooling. The gap is too fundamental to bridge with general engineering skill.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) demonstrated consistently across multiple roles
Genuine interest in developer tooling — contributed to test frameworks, MCP code generators, and organized coding dojos and workshops
Broad polyglot background with demonstrated ability to ramp on unfamiliar technologies quickly
concerns: No credible recent Java or Kotlin experience; primary stack for the past 7+ years is TypeScript/Node.js
Zero Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience anywhere in the resume — the core technical requirement of the role
No evidence of JVM internals knowledge, IDE plugin development, or enterprise Java tooling of any kind

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
